name: Ernesto Nazareth
description:
'''Ernesto Júlio de Nazareth''' (20 March 1863 - 1 February 1934) was a Brazilian [[composer]] and [[pianist]]. He was one of the central figures in the [[piano]] music of [[Rio de Janeiro]] at the turn of the twentieth century, and his work helped shape the passage between nineteenth-century [[Salon music|salon dance music]] and early urban [[Music of Brazil|Brazilian popular music]].

His compositions are closely associated with [[Maxixe (dance)|maxixe]], [[Brazilian tango]] and [[choro]], and draw on [[polka]], [[Lundu (dance)|lundu]], [[Habanera (music)|habanera]] and other dance forms, set within idiomatic piano writing shaped by his [[classical music|classical]] training.

Nazareth published many of his works as tangos, waltzes, polkas and other popular genres, but his music has remained in the repertory of pianists, choro musicians and scholars of Brazilian music. Works such as ''Brejeiro'', ''[[Odeon (Nazareth)|Odeon]]'', ''[[Apanhei-te, cavaquinho]]'' and ''Ameno Resedá'' became among his best-known compositions. He was later named patron of chair no. 28 of the [[Brazilian Academy of Music]].
